meaning: adj.虚的,实质的,[物]有效的,事实上的
examples: The company has a virtual monopoly in this area of trade.
Up to four players can compete in a virtual world of role playing.
One day virtual reality will revolutionize the entertainment industry.
Eventually, someone will figure out that this is just the tip of a virtual iceberg.
The update also lays the groundwork for the imminent launch of the Wii U Virtual Console service.
